channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Women's soccer stars announce pregnancy, team makes statement on alleged 'abuse' to them days later
item.description: Soccer players Sam Kerr and Kristie Mewis announced that Mewis is pregnant, and then Chelsea FC issued a statement addressing abuse against them for it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An emotional Rafael Nadal retires at the Davis Cup after he loses and Spain is eliminated
item.description: MALAGA, Spain (AP) — Rafael Nadal bit his lower lip and his reddened eyes welled with tears as he stood alongside his Davis Cup teammates for Spain's national anthem Tuesday before what he — and everyone — knew might be the last match of his career.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Colorado superfan Miss Peggy shares message about late twin sister on 100th birthday after Buffaloes win
item.description: Colorado superfan Peggy Coppom, or Miss Peggy as fans call her, celebrated her 100th birthday with head coach Deion Sanders at a press conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tampa Bay Rays say new St. Pete stadium is unlikely to be ready for 2028 season, if at all
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — A combination of severe hurricane damage to Tropicana Field and political delays on financing means it is highly unlikely the Tampa Bay Rays' planned new stadium will be ready for the 2028 season, if at all, the team said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bruins fire coach Jim Montgomery after slow start in regular season follows playoff disappointments
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Boston coach Jim Montgomery, who led the Bruins to the greatest regular season in NHL history but never got them past the second round of the playoffs, was fired Tuesday, a day after the below-.500 team lost to last-place Columbus to extend its losing streak to three games.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jake Paul-Mike Tyson was highest-grossing combat event in Texas at $18.1M, organizers say
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Jake Paul's unanimous decision over Mike Tyson was the highest-grossing combat sports event in Texas at $18.1 million, the promotions company co-founded by Paul said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jake Paul-Mike Tyson was highest-grossing combat event in Texas at $18.1M, organizers say
item.description: Jake Paul’s promotions company says the fighter's unanimous decision over Mike Tyson was the highest-grossing combat sports event in Texas at $18.1 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NHL referee Mitch Dunning is home and expects to make a full recovery, league says
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The NHL referee who was stretchered out of a game following a violent collision with Colorado defenseman Josh Manson was back home Tuesday and expected to make a full recovery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Taylor Swift fans attack Bills player's wife for bragging about beating Chiefs, mocking singer
item.description: Taylor Swift fans went after their latest target, the wife of Bills linebacker Baylon Spector, after she bragged about beating the Chiefs this past weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bob Love, a 3-time NBA All-Star with the Chicago Bulls, has died at 81
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former Chicago Bulls forward Bob Love, a three-time All-Star who spent 11 years in the NBA, died Monday. He was 81. The Bulls said Love died after a long battle with cancer. Love averaged 21.3 points and 6.8 rebounds during his nine seasons with the team. “With his No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spurs star Wembanyama will miss game against Thunder as he recovers from bruised right knee
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — San Antonio Spurs center Victor Wembanyama will not play against the Oklahoma City Thunder on Tuesday night as he recovers from a bruised right knee. San Antonio guard Devin Vassell was listed as questionable for the NBA Cup game with a sore left knee.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL has 'no issue' with Trump dance celebrations amid recent trend
item.description: The NFL confirmed to Fox News Digital on Tuesday it had "no issue" with players emulating President-elect Trump with their celebratory dances.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pulisic scores twice, US beats Jamaica 4-2 for 5-2 aggregate win in CONCACAF Nations League QF
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Mauricio Pochettino had a message for American players after a 5-2 aggregate win over Jamaica gave the three-time defending champion United States a berth in the CONCACAF Nations League semifinals. “My advice is look after yourself.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Debris falls from roof at home of Cowboys before game against Texans
item.description: A piece of the roof at the home of the Dallas Cowboys fell to the field while the retractable portion of it was being opened at least three hours before a 34-10 loss to Houston on Monday night, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cowboys fall at home again after piece of roof comes down before loss to Texans
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Things were literally falling from the sky even before the Dallas Cowboys lost yet another home game. The roof at AT&T Stadium can be fixed.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texans never trail while handing Cowboys 5th straight loss, 34-10
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Derek Barnett forced the first fumble and returned the second for a touchdown on the definitive defensive play of the game for the Houston Texans. Joe Mixon did his thing, and C.J. Stroud didn’t have to do much to extend the misery of the Dallas Cowboys.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bulls great Bob Love dead at 81 after battle with cancer
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — An all-star for the Chicago Bulls in the 1970's, franchise great Bob Love has died at the age of 81 after a long battle with cancer, the team announced Monday. Love played from 1968 to 1976 with the Chicago Bulls and was affectionately nicknamed "Butterbean" for his kind demeanor.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Bird, Fowles, Pondexter, Beard headline Women's Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2025
item.description: WNBA greats Sue Bird, Sylvia Fowles, Cappie Pondexter and Alana Beard headline the Women's Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2025 that was announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Curry upset after Klay 'ghosted' him before game, Draymond Green says
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- While it seemed to be all sunshine and roses inside Chase Center as the packed arena welcomed back Klay Thompson on Tuesday, that wasn't exactly the case -- at least amongst the players on the court.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: USC women’s basketball team beats Cal State Northridge by 85 points
item.description: The USC Trojans women’s basketball team was not messing around Tuesday night, shattering the team record for most points scored in a single game. Led by standout JuJu Watkins (21 points, nine assists, six steals), the Trojans beat the Matadors of Cal State Northridge by a score of 124-39.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5
